objective: C3A-CP6 implements the next Checkpoint 3A child checkpoint: an executable security/observability probe that executes the local product/API path and evaluates runtime API-visible security controls, privacy/redaction behavior, observability metadata, bounded failure evidence, and anti-false-pass guarantees. Positive claims: C3A-CP6-HARNESS-001 dispatches the security/observability probe as an implemented executable pytest probe with shell=False and bounded/redacted failure output while preserving CP1 API E2E, CP2 output-correctness, CP3 language-quality, CP4 media-artifacts, and CP5 access/quota/retention probes; C3A-CP6-SEC-001 creates approved synthetic local project knowledge through API behavior, ingests/chunks/stores it, generates grounded walkthrough output, exercises unapproved upload and missing-approval failure, prompt-injection or instruction-override handling, unsupported-claim evaluation, cross-project and mismatched replay/access scope, idempotency replay boundaries, and public-safe redaction for raw unsafe content and sensitive markers; C3A-CP6-OBS-001 verifies runtime trace/run/request/evaluation metadata, bounded status/failure summaries, deterministic redaction, runtime run binding, local/mock provider posture, and enough diagnostic evidence without leaking raw payloads; C3A-CP6-NEG-001 proves docs/prose/static-snapshot, status-only, canned-success, success-shaped dict, missing nonce/source/run binding, cross-project replay, idempotency bypass, missing observability binding, and weak redaction behavior inadequate. Later performance and real-browser E2E probes remain planned and do not complete Checkpoint 3A. Repository-ledger cleanup for PR #264 and issue #263 may be bundled because this is a substantive child checkpoint rather than a standalone status-only follow-up.
